name: Automation Builder — create workflow (write)
description: Covers the create-workflow state mutation as an Unlock-2 effect:write transition, against the empty fixture project (spec-ci-fixture) selected via ?project=<id> (this spec id is in GATED_SPECS, so routeForSpec appends the seeded fixture id). The structural state asserts the empty-editor baseline ('No Workflow Selected' + 'Create New Workflow' + 'Automation Library'). The CREATE write clicks 'Create New Workflow'; the builder creates a sequential workflow, auto-selects it, and renders it as a library item with a delete control — the waitAfter asserts that delete control (aria-label 'Delete workflow') APPEARS, proving the workflow now exists. The new workflow auto-saves into the fixture project's configuration. REVERSIBILITY/CLEANUP: the write is reverted at the harness level, not in-spec — the idempotent pre-run baselineReset() PUTs the fixture project's configuration -> {} at the START of every run (run-spec-ci.ts), which deletes any workflow this spec created, so the shared ci-bot account self-heals and never accumulates state across runs. An in-spec UI delete (library trash -> 'Delete Workflow' confirm dialog) was attempted but is NOT reliably relay-drivable: a relay-dispatched click on the Radix AlertDialogAction confirm button dismisses the modal WITHOUT firing its onConfirm, so the workflow is never deleted (verified repeatedly in CI: post-click the dialog is gone yet the workflow + its delete control remain). Rather than ship a flaky transition on the REQUIRED gate, the create write is reverted by the deterministic start-of-run reset. effect:write (reversible: the project-config reset restores the empty baseline). NOT destructive. Concurrency/blast-radius safety: each run's baselineReset wipes the shared fixture project to empty before creating, and this waitAfter reads client-side DOM (context-isolated per run), so concurrent PR runs cannot cross-contaminate the gate; the project converges to empty after every run.

states.name: Empty editor baseline
states.description: The automation-builder empty editor for the (empty) fixture project: 'No Workflow Selected' heading, the 'Create New Workflow' CTA, and the 'Automation Library' panel heading. This is the start-state the create write operates from and the state the start-of-run reset restores.

assertions.description: 'No Workflow Selected' empty-editor heading is rendered.
'Create New Workflow' action is present in the empty editor.
Automation Library panel heading is present alongside the builder.

transitions.name: Create a workflow (write)
transitions.description: Behavioral WRITE: click 'Create New Workflow' (#button-create-new-workflow) in the empty editor. The builder creates a new sequential workflow, auto-selects it (the empty editor unmounts), and renders it as a library item with a delete control. The waitAfter IS the assertion: the library item's delete control (aria-label 'Delete workflow') APPEARS — proving the create succeeded (the workflow now exists in the library). effect:write — the new workflow auto-saves into the fixture project's configuration; it is reverted by the start-of-run baselineReset (PUT config={}), which keeps the shared account clean across runs. activateStates is empty: the post-create surface is the workflow editor, not a second always-required structural state.
